NKJV.1: A Psalm of David. Give unto the LORD, O you mighty ones, Give unto the LORD glory and strength.
NKJV.2: Give unto the LORD the glory due to His name; Worship the LORD in the beauty of holiness.
NKJV.3: The voice of the LORD is over the waters; The God of glory thunders; The LORD is over many waters.
NKJV.4: The voice of the LORD is powerful; The voice of the LORD is full of majesty.
NKJV.5: The voice of the LORD breaks the cedars, Yes, the LORD splinters the cedars of Lebanon.
NKJV.6: He makes them also skip like a calf, Lebanon and Sirion like a young wild ox.
NKJV.7: The voice of the LORD divides the flames of fire.
NKJV.8: The voice of the LORD shakes the wilderness; The LORD shakes the Wilderness of Kadesh.
NKJV.9: The voice of the LORD makes the deer give birth, And strips the forests bare; And in His temple everyone says, “Glory!”
NKJV.10: The LORD sat enthroned at the Flood, And the LORD sits as King forever.
NKJV.11: The LORD will give strength to His people; The LORD will bless His people with peace.

KJV.1: A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. Give<S>3051</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S>, O ye mighty<S>1121</S> <S>410</S>, give<S>3051</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S> glory<S>3519</S> and strength<S>5797</S>. <sup>ye mighty: Heb. ye sons of the mighty</sup>
KJV.2: Give<S>3051</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S> the glory<S>3519</S> due unto his name<S>8034</S>; worship<S>7812</S> the LORD<S>3068</S> in the beauty<S>1927</S> of holiness<S>6944</S>. <sup>the glory: Heb. the honour of his name</sup> <sup>in: or, in his glorious sanctuary</sup>
KJV.3: The voice<S>6963</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> is upon the waters<S>4325</S>: the God<S>410</S> of glory<S>3519</S> thundereth<S>7481</S>: the LORD<S>3068</S> is upon many<S>7227</S> waters<S>4325</S>. <sup>many: or, great</sup>
KJV.4: The voice<S>6963</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> is powerful<S>3581</S>; the voice<S>6963</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> is full of majesty<S>1926</S>. <sup>powerful: Heb. in power</sup> <sup>full: Heb. in majesty</sup>
KJV.5: The voice<S>6963</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> breaketh<S>7665</S> the cedars<S>730</S>; yea, the LORD<S>3068</S> breaketh<S>7665</S> the cedars<S>730</S> of Lebanon<S>3844</S>.
KJV.6: He maketh them also to skip<S>7540</S> like a calf<S>5695</S>; Lebanon<S>3844</S> and Sirion<S>8303</S> like a young<S>1121</S> unicorn<S>7214</S>.
KJV.7: The voice<S>6963</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> divideth<S>2672</S> the flames<S>3852</S> of fire<S>784</S>. <sup>divideth: Heb. cutteth out</sup>
KJV.8: The voice<S>6963</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> shaketh<S>2342</S> the wilderness<S>4057</S>; the LORD<S>3068</S> shaketh<S>2342</S> the wilderness<S>4057</S> of Kadesh<S>6946</S>.
KJV.9: The voice<S>6963</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> maketh the hinds<S>355</S> to calve<S>2342</S>, and discovereth<S>2834</S> the forests<S>3295</S>: and in his temple<S>1964</S> doth every one speak<S>559</S> of his glory<S>3519</S>. <sup>to calve: or, to be in pain</sup> <sup>doth: or, every whit of it uttereth, etc</sup>
KJV.10: The LORD<S>3068</S> sitteth<S>3427</S> upon the flood<S>3999</S>; yea, the LORD<S>3068</S> sitteth<S>3427</S> King<S>4428</S> for ever<S>5769</S>.
KJV.11: The LORD<S>3068</S> will give<S>5414</S> strength<S>5797</S> unto his people<S>5971</S>; the LORD<S>3068</S> will bless<S>1288</S> his people<S>5971</S> with peace<S>7965</S>.

ESV.1: Ascribe to the LORD, O heavenly beings,  ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.
ESV.2: Ascribe to the LORD the glory due his name; worship the LORD in the splendor of holiness.
ESV.3: The voice of the LORD is over the waters; the God of glory thunders, the LORD, over many waters.
ESV.4: The voice of the LORD is powerful; the voice of the LORD is full of majesty.
ESV.5: The voice of the LORD breaks the cedars; the LORD breaks the cedars of Lebanon.
ESV.6: He makes Lebanon to skip like a calf, and Sirion like a young wild ox.
ESV.7: The voice of the LORD flashes forth flames of fire.
ESV.8: The voice of the LORD shakes the wilderness; the LORD shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.
ESV.9: The voice of the LORD makes the deer give birth  and strips the forests bare, and in his temple all cry, “Glory!”
ESV.10: The LORD sits enthroned over the flood; the LORD sits enthroned as king forever.
ESV.11: May the LORD give strength to his people! May the LORD bless  his people with peace!

NASB.1: A Psalm of David. Ascribe to the LORD, O sons of the mighty, Ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.
NASB.2: Ascribe to the LORD the glory due to His name; Worship the LORD in holy array.
NASB.3: The voice of the LORD is upon the waters; The God of glory thunders, The LORD is over many waters.
NASB.4: The voice of the LORD is powerful, The voice of the LORD is majestic.
NASB.5: The voice of the LORD breaks the cedars; Yes, the LORD breaks in pieces the cedars of Lebanon.
NASB.6: He makes Lebanon skip like a calf, And Sirion like a young wild ox.
NASB.7: The voice of the LORD hews out flames of fire.
NASB.8: The voice of the LORD shakes the wilderness; The LORD shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.
NASB.9: The voice of the LORD makes the deer to calve And strips the forests bare; And in His temple everything says, 'Glory!'
NASB.10: The LORD sat [as King] at the flood; Yes, the LORD sits as King forever.
NASB.11: The LORD will give strength to His people; The LORD will bless His people with peace.

NLT.1:
Honor the LORD, you heavenly beings ;
honor the LORD for his glory and strength.
NLT.2:
Honor the LORD for the glory of his name.
Worship the LORD in the splendor of his holiness.
NLT.3:
The voice of the LORD echoes above the sea.
The God of glory thunders.
The LORD thunders over the mighty sea.
NLT.4:
The voice of the LORD is powerful;
the voice of the LORD is majestic.
NLT.5:
The voice of the LORD splits the mighty cedars;
the LORD shatters the cedars of Lebanon.
NLT.6:
He makes Lebanon’s mountains skip like a calf;
he makes Mount Hermon leap like a young wild ox.
NLT.7:
The voice of the LORD strikes
with bolts of lightning.
NLT.8:
The voice of the LORD makes the barren wilderness quake;
the LORD shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.
NLT.9:
The voice of the LORD twists mighty oaks
and strips the forests bare.
In his Temple everyone shouts, “Glory!”
NLT.10:
The LORD rules over the floodwaters.
The LORD reigns as king forever.
NLT.11:
The LORD gives his people strength.
The LORD blesses them with peace.

WEB.1: A Psalm by David. Ascribe to Yahweh, you sons of the mighty, ascribe to Yahweh glory and strength.
WEB.2: Ascribe to Yahweh the glory due to his name. Worship Yahweh in holy array.
WEB.3: Yahweh’s voice is on the waters. The God of glory thunders, even Yahweh on many waters.
WEB.4: Yahweh’s voice is powerful. Yahweh’s voice is full of majesty.
WEB.5: Yahweh’s voice breaks the cedars. Yes, Yahweh breaks in pieces the cedars of Lebanon.
WEB.6: He makes them also to skip like a calf; Lebanon and Sirion like a young, wild ox.
WEB.7: Yahweh’s voice strikes with flashes of lightning.
WEB.8: Yahweh’s voice shakes the wilderness. Yahweh shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.
WEB.9: Yahweh’s voice makes the deer calve, and strips the forests bare. In his temple everything says, “Glory!”
WEB.10: Yahweh sat enthroned at the Flood. Yes, Yahweh sits as King forever.
WEB.11: Yahweh will give strength to his people. Yahweh will bless his people with peace.

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David. Ascribe to the LORD, O sons of the mighty,
Ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.
AMP.2:
Ascribe to the LORD the glory due His name;
Worship the LORD in the beauty and majesty of His holiness [as the creator and source of holiness].
AMP.3:
The voice of the LORD is upon the waters;
The God of glory thunders;
The LORD is over many waters.
AMP.4:
The voice of the LORD is powerful;
The voice of the LORD is full of majesty.
AMP.5:
The voice of the LORD breaks the cedars;
Yes, the LORD breaks in pieces the cedars of Lebanon.
AMP.6:
He makes Lebanon skip like a calf,
And Sirion (Mount Hermon) like a young, wild ox.
AMP.7: The voice of the LORD rakes flames of fire (lightning).
AMP.8:
The voice of the LORD shakes the wilderness;
The LORD shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.
AMP.9:
The voice of the LORD makes the doe labor and give birth
And strips the forests bare;
And in His temple all are saying, "Glory!"
AMP.10:
The LORD sat as King at the flood;
Yes, the LORD sits as King forever.
AMP.11:
The LORD will give [unyielding and impenetrable] strength to His people;
The LORD will bless His people with peace.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Bravo, God, bravo!
Gods and all angels shout, "Encore!"
MSG.2:
In awe before the glory,
in awe before God's visible power.
Stand at attention!
Dress your best to honor him!
MSG.3:
God thunders across the waters,
Brilliant, his voice and his face, streaming brightness—God, across the flood waters.
MSG.5:
God's thunder smashes cedars,
God topples the northern cedars.
MSG.6:
The mountain ranges skip like spring colts,
The high ridges jump like wild kid goats.
MSG.8:
God thunders, the wilderness quakes;
He makes the desert of Kadesh shake.
MSG.9:
God's thunder sets the oak trees dancing
A wild dance, whirling; the pelting rain strips their branches.
We fall to our knees—we call out, "Glory!"
MSG.10:
Above the floodwaters is God's throne
from which his power flows,
from which he rules the world.
MSG.11:
God makes his people strong.
God gives his people peace.

NIV.1:
Psalm 29
A psalm of David.
Ascribe to the Lord, O mighty ones,
ascribe to the Lord glory and strength.
NIV.2:
Ascribe to the Lord the glory due his name;
worship the Lord in the splendor of his holiness.
NIV.3:
The voice of the Lord is over the waters;
the God of glory thunders,
the Lord thunders over the mighty waters.
NIV.4:
The voice of the Lord is powerful;
the voice of the Lord is majestic.
NIV.5:
The voice of the Lord breaks the cedars;
the Lord breaks in pieces the cedars of Lebanon.
NIV.6:
He makes Lebanon skip like a calf,
Sirion like a young wild ox.
NIV.7:
The voice of the Lord strikes
with flashes of lightning.
NIV.8:
The voice of the Lord shakes the desert;
the Lord shakes the Desert of Kadesh.
NIV.9:
The voice of the Lord twists the oaks
and strips the forests bare.
And in his temple all cry, “Glory!”
NIV.10:
The Lord sits enthroned over the flood;
the Lord is enthroned as King forever.
NIV.11:
The Lord gives strength to his people;
the Lord blesses his people with peace.

NRSVCE.1:
Ascribe to the LORD, O heavenly beings,
ascribe to the LORD glory and strength.
NRSVCE.2:

worship the LORD in holy splendor.
NRSVCE.3:

the God of glory thunders,

NRSVCE.5:

NET.1: Acknowledge the LORD, you heavenly beings, acknowledge the LORD’s majesty and power!
NET.2: Acknowledge the majesty of the LORD’s reputation! Worship the LORD in holy attire!
NET.3: The LORD’s shout is heard over the water; the majestic God thunders, the LORD appears over the surging water.
NET.4: The LORD’s shout is powerful, the LORD’s shout is majestic.
NET.5: The LORD’s shout breaks the cedars, the LORD shatters the cedars of Lebanon.
NET.6: He makes Lebanon skip like a calf and Sirion like a young ox.
NET.7: The LORD’s shout strikes with flaming fire.
NET.8: The LORD’s shout shakes the wilderness, the LORD shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.
NET.9: The LORD’s shout bends the large trees and strips the leaves from the forests. Everyone in his temple says, “Majestic!”
NET.10: The LORD sits enthroned over the engulfing waters, the LORD sits enthroned as the eternal king.
NET.11: The LORD gives his people strength; the LORD grants his people security.
